PROPOSED STAGE 1 / 17
OrangeBD · OneGov Framework Whole-of-Government Architecture · onegov.global

The Platform Behind DGTP

ONEGOV FRAMEWORK — HOW IT WORKS
Citizen Interface▼
Citizens reach government through any channel. A single eID (GovPass) authenticates once and carries across every service — no re-login per department.
OneGov Framework (OGF) Core▼
The OGF is the engine room. Service Builder defines workflows, Workflow Engine executes them with SLA clocks, Admin Console gives management visibility, and Reporting & Analytics closes the loop.
Gov Interface — Officer Workspace▼
Officers authenticate once with GovID and see every system through one Gov Interface. Case context, registry data, and documents are pre-loaded before any citizen interaction.
National Data Registries▼
OGF connects to all registries via the Mediator Engine — Civil, Business, Address, Land, Tax, Licensing. No registry is replaced. All exchanges logged with a correlationId.
Departmental Backend Systems▼
Existing department systems — Education, Health, Land, Finance, Energy — stay operational. OGF wraps each in a consistent API layer and orchestrates events without touching legacy code.
Connect Service Bus & Mediator Engine▼
The Connect Service Bus is the durable event backbone. The Mediator Engine handles protocol translation and consent enforcement. Together they are what DGTP's Q1 decision resolves.
OGF Modules — Plug-in Capabilities▼
Eight plug-in modules activate per deployment. For DGTP Sint Maarten, GovSign, GovPay, and GovPush are directly relevant to the six architecture decisions.
The 5L Design Principles▼
Every OGF deployment is governed by five design principles — the 5Ls — embedded from day one, not bolted on.
The Story How these 6 questions emerged · Click to advance

From a Government Challenge to Six Architecture Decisions

Sint Maarten needed to digitise government services for 50,000+ citizens. But the architecture challenge was complex. Click to begin the story.
Findings & Gap Analysis · 1 of 3TOGAF Business Architecture · 6 Registries Assessed

Executive Summary

6 key registries analysed. Average capability maturity of 2.1/5 — widespread Ad-hoc operations, manual processes, and no performance monitoring across all departments.

▲ Avg 2.1
MATURITY SCORE SCALE (1–5)
1 Initial Unpredictable, uncontrolled, reactive — no real structure
2 Ad-hoc Some planning exists, but processes are not yet standardised
3 Defined Processes are documented and standardised across the team
4 Managed Processes are measured, controlled and data-driven
5 Optimised Continuous improvement enabled by data and innovation
SYSTEMIC FINDINGS — COMMON TO ALL 6 REGISTRIES
F1
Manual & paper-based processes — All 6 registries operate on paper, manual data entry, or spreadsheets with no integrated digital workflow.
Impact: duplicate data entry, errors, zero auditability
F2
Leadership instability — Multiple registries operate without permanent leadership, causing strategic drift and governance bottlenecks.
Impact: delayed decisions, no digital roadmap ownership
F3
Absent or legacy digital platforms — Where IT exists it is standalone, fragmented, or outdated (MS Access, legacy CRM, no e-filing, no API).
Impact: no interoperability, data silos, security risk
F4
No inter-departmental integration — Each registry operates in isolation; cross-registry verification is entirely manual, slow, and error-prone.
Impact: citizens re-submit same data at every office
F5
No performance monitoring — Absence of KPIs, dashboards, or SLA frameworks across all registries. No accountability or improvement loop.
Impact: strategic blindness, no investment justification
F6
Legislative & compliance gaps — UBO registry lacks legislative backing. No Privacy Ordinance compliance infrastructure exists. No WORM audit trail.
Impact: legal exposure, non-compliance risk
Findings & Gap Analysis · 2 of 3Registry-by-registry · Swipe or use arrows

Six Registries — Identified Gaps

Each registry independently assessed. Navigate through each to explore gaps and what breaks without integration.

Findings & Gap Analysis · 3 of 3Cross-cutting gaps → Six architecture decisions

From Every Gap — to Six Decisions

All 20+ registry gaps trace back to six systemic architecture gaps. Resolving these six — in the right order — fixes them all.

CROSS-CUTTING GAP — INTEGRATION
No Shared Gateway — 6 Registries, 6 Silos
Every cross-registry verification gap (Civil↔Land↔Tax↔Business↔Address) stems from the same root: no shared API gateway. Kadaster alone has 8 manual integration gaps with other registries. Shared Solutions (eSignature, CX Pay, Sentoo, CRM) also cannot connect without 24 additional bespoke integrations.
Kadaster: 8 manual linksTax: no sync24 bespoke integrations neededResolved by Q1
CROSS-CUTTING GAP — CITIZEN SERVICES
All Services Counter-Based — No Digital Citizen Journey
All 6 registries require citizens to visit in person. No appointment self-serve. No proactive notifications. No application status visibility. The 2023 NRD confirmed this as the top citizen complaint — and it is the same complaint across all 6 registries.
All 6: no self-serveNRD 2023 #1 complaintResolved by Q2 + Q3
CROSS-CUTTING GAP — COMPLIANCE
No Audit Trail — Legal & Privacy Ordinance Exposure
Officer data access in Civil, Tax, Land and Business registries leaves no auditable record. UBO registry lacks legislative backing. No Privacy Ordinance compliance infrastructure. No WORM storage. Any appeal or legal challenge has no evidential basis — from any registry.
Privacy Ordinance breachUBO: no legal backingResolved by Q4
CROSS-CUTTING GAP — GOVERNANCE
No SLA Clocks, No KPIs — Blindness Across All Registries
All 6 registries lack formal KPIs, SLA frameworks, or dashboards. Tax Administration IT scores 1/5. Land Registry Data Integration scores 1/5. Backlogs are invisible. No escalation path exists. No data to demonstrate or justify programme value to SteerCo.
All 6: no KPIsTax IT: 1/5 maturityResolved by Q5 + Q6
THEREFORE —
Six Decisions.
All Open.
Every gap across all 6 registries is resolvable through six architecture decisions. Each presents multiple options — no technology is pre-selected. These are the six questions that block or enable the entire programme.
Q1Integration Data Bus — How do 6 registries talk securely?›
Q2Appointment System — Qmatic + QConnect Bridge›
Q3Notification Service — Push + Pull, every event, every channel›
Q4Application Traceability — Immutable WORM audit trail›
Q5Application Lifecycle — 8-state BPMN with SLA governance›
Q6Citizen Happiness Index — Daily CXI score, target ≥80›
Navigate forward to evaluate each decision with options, trade-offs, and proposed architecture.
OverviewAll six decisions · Proposed stage

Six Decisions. All Open.

Each question evaluated with multiple options, trade-offs, and architecture flows. No technology is pre-selected.

Important: DGTP is in the proposed stage. These are recommendations for evaluation — not deployed decisions. Final architecture will be agreed with all stakeholders before any commitment.

PROPOSED STAGE 2 / 10
Decision 01 — Integration Data BusQ1 of 6

How Do Six Registries Talk to Each Other?

USER STORY"As a registry admin, I need all six registries to exchange data through a single secure gateway — auditable, versioned, and extensible without breaking existing integrations."
OPTIONS EVALUATED
AAzure APIM + Service Bus★ PREFERRED · ILLUSTRATIVE
Managed gateway — zero patching, auto-scaled
SOAP-to-REST policy for Civil Registry StUF-BG
Azure platform dependency
Cost scales with API call volume
BWSO2 API Manager (open-source)
Open standards, full code ownership
SOAP + REST + GraphQL support
Ops team required for all maintenance
Manual certificate expiry risk
CX-Road (Nordic federated model)
Open source, proven at national scale
Strong security model, battle-tested EU
Designed for multi-sovereign federation
On-premise security servers required
6
Registries
0
Point-to-point
100%
Auditable
PREFERRED (ILLUSTRATIVE) →Azure APIM + Service Bus — subject to DGTP stakeholder agreement
SXM CONTEXT
Sint Maarten has 6 registries on 6 different platforms — CASBES (StUF-BG SOAP), Oracle (OData), Dynamics 365, ArcGIS, SIAH (SFTP), and Kadaster. A single gateway that handles all protocols eliminates 15 point-to-point integrations.
KEY RISK WITHOUT THIS
Without a unified gateway, each new service requires bespoke registry integrations. The current K2DD→CASBES path is already a single point of failure — no gateway means no circuit breaker and no fallback when registries go down.
IMMEDIATE WIN (SPRINT 2)
Mock policies let all 6 development streams run in parallel before any registry is live. Teams don't block each other. This alone saves an estimated 8–12 weeks of integration delays.
KEY DEPENDENCY
Decision directly affects Q3 (notifications routing), Q4 (event sourcing backbone), and Q5 (workflow orchestration). Agree this first — everything else connects through the gateway.
★ PREFERRED OPTION (ILLUSTRATIVE) — AZURE APIM + AZURE SERVICE BUS
PROPOSED STAGE 3 / 10
⚠ TECHNOLOGY DECISION NOTE No RabbitMQ, Kafka, NATS, ActiveMQ, Pulsar, or any other third-party message broker is used. The Q1 decision is exclusively Azure Service Bus Premium — durable ordered delivery, retry with exponential backoff, dead-letter queue, circuit breaker, and ETag cache invalidation events. All event flows in this programme route through Azure Service Bus.
Decision 02 — Appointment SystemQ2 of 6

Extending Qmatic with a Smart Integration Layer

Qmatic has real-time scheduling and live slots — but cannot self-serve reschedule. A dedicated integration layer bridges this gap without replacing the existing system.

QMATIC TODAY Qmatic provides real-time schedule slots for SXM government appointments. However, it cannot allow citizens to self-reschedule — service providers must call citizens a day before to confirm. There is no SMS two-way interaction, no case linkage, and no QR kiosk check-in.
NEW
QConnect — Qmatic Integration Bridge
A lightweight integration service that sits between the citizen portal and Qmatic — adding the missing capabilities while preserving the existing scheduling infrastructure.
RESCHEDULE ENGINE
SMS two-way rescheduling — citizen replies RESCHEDULE, QConnect fetches live Qmatic slots and books new time automatically
CASE LINKAGE
Every Qmatic booking linked to active service case via correlationId — officer pre-briefed automatically on arrival
QR KIOSK CHECK-IN
Citizen scans QR on arrival — Qmatic queue updated, case state advanced, officer dashboard notified in real time
REMINDER CHAIN
Automated D-1 reminder via SMS + push — replacing the manual officer call. Citizen confirms or reschedules via reply
0
Qmatic replaced
68%
No-shows addressed
3–4m
Saved per appt
D-1
Auto reminder
PROPOSED →QConnect bridge preserves Qmatic investment while adding SMS reschedule, case linkage & QR check-in
QCONNECT INTEGRATION ARCHITECTURE — QMATIC BRIDGE (ILLUSTRATIVE)
QMATIC CONTEXT
Qmatic already provides real-time slot availability — this is not a gap. The gap is self-serve rescheduling. Officers currently call citizens manually the day before to confirm. QConnect automates this entirely.
68% NO-SHOW DRIVER
In comparable programmes, 68% of no-shows are caused by inability to reschedule without calling. SMS two-way rescheduling via QConnect directly eliminates this — without touching Qmatic.
NO SYSTEM REPLACEMENT
QConnect is an integration layer, not a replacement. Qmatic continues to own scheduling, queues and slot management. QConnect adds the missing citizen-facing capabilities on top via API.
CASE LINKAGE VALUE
Linking every Qmatic appointment to the active case via correlationId means officers arrive pre-briefed — saving 3–4 minutes per appointment and eliminating manual post-appointment case updates.
Decision 03 — Notification ServiceQ3 of 6

Push When Something Happens. Pull When You Want to Know.

Two complementary notification modes — proactive push on every event, and on-demand pull via a live status API — so citizens are never left waiting or guessing.

USER STORY "As a citizen, I want a notification every time anything changes — and when I open my portal, I want to see exactly where my application is right now, without waiting for something to be sent to me."
📤 PUSH NOTIFICATION
Government tells you
Triggered automatically on every state change — Email, SMS, Push, In-App. Citizen does nothing. 13+ event types covered.
Event-driven Zero citizen action WORM audited
📥 PULL NOTIFICATION
You ask, we answer
A Status API that returns the live application state, full history timeline, and next expected step — on demand, any time the citizen opens the portal.
REST GET endpoint Live progress view JWT secured
HOW PULL WORKS — STATUS API
GET /api/citizen/applications/{correlationId}/status — returns current state, last updated timestamp, and next expected action
GET /api/citizen/applications/{correlationId}/timeline — returns full immutable event history from WORM store, viewable as a progress stepper in the portal
SSE /api/citizen/applications/{correlationId}/live — Server-Sent Events stream for real-time updates while the portal tab is open, without polling
PUSH CHANNEL OPTIONS
AAzure ACS + Status API (Azure APIM)★ PROPOSED
Push: Email + SMS + FCM Push in one SDK
Pull: Status API natively via Azure APIM gateway
Azure platform dependency
SSE requires APIM streaming policy config
BTwilio / SendGrid + Custom Status API
Push: platform-agnostic, portable
Pull: custom REST API, full control
Two separate systems to integrate and audit
SSE needs separate WebSocket/SSE server
CGov Notify Module (Push only)
Pre-integrated push with workflow events
Single audit trail by design
No native Pull / Status API capability
Citizen must wait to be notified — no on-demand view
4
Push channels
3
Pull endpoints
13+
Event triggers
EN+PAP
Languages
PROPOSED →Azure ACS (Push) + Status API via APIM (Pull) — fully audited, citizen-initiated or event-driven
PUSH + PULL NOTIFICATION ARCHITECTURE — OPTION A (ILLUSTRATIVE)
Decision 04 — Application TraceabilityQ4 of 6

Every Action. Immutable. End-to-End.

USER STORY"As an officer, I need a tamper-proof log of every action. As a citizen, I want a visual timeline showing exactly where my application is and what happens next."
OPTIONS EVALUATED
AEvent Sourcing + WORM Blob + APM
Immutable by design — cannot be altered
Full distributed trace via correlationId
Higher storage cost over time
Event schema discipline required
BAudit Table in RDBMS
Simple, fast to implement
Already part of dev team stack
Mutable — can be altered or deleted
No distributed cross-service trace
CBlockchain / DLT Ledger
Cryptographic immutability
Decentralised trust model
High complexity and infrastructure cost
Governance model unclear for SXM
7 yr
Legal retention
1 ID
All services traced
3
Audience views
PROPOSED →Event Sourcing + Azure WORM Blob + App Insights — storage cost to agree with DGTP
SXM CONTEXT
Currently there is no end-to-end audit trail across the 6 registries. An officer accessing civil registry data leaves no auditable record. This is both a compliance gap and a governance risk — particularly for sensitive personal data under the Privacy Ordinance.
APPEALS & LEGAL PROTECTION
An immutable audit log is the single most important asset in a citizen appeal. Without it, the government cannot prove what an officer did, when, and on what basis. Option A (WORM) provides this by design — Option B (RDBMS) does not.
RDBMS AUDIT IS MUTABLE
Option B audit tables in MSSQL can be altered by a DBA or via SQL. This means they cannot serve as legal evidence. If a citizen challenges a decision, a mutable audit log has no standing. WORM storage is legally defensible; RDBMS audit is not.
CORRELATIONID IS THE KEY
A single UUID assigned at submission and carried through every registry call, notification, and payment enables a complete citizen journey reconstruction in one query. This only works if every service emits events — agree the event schema early.
PROPOSED TRACEABILITY ARCHITECTURE — OPTION A (ILLUSTRATIVE)
PROPOSED STAGE 6 / 10
Decision 05 — Application LifecycleQ5 of 6

From Submission to Archival. Governed.

USER STORY"As a supervisor, I need every application to follow a defined workflow with SLA clocks and automatic escalation — so nothing falls through the cracks."
OPTIONS EVALUATED
ABPMN Engine (Camunda / Flowable)
Visual process design, no-code changes
SLA clocks and escalation built in
Ops overhead for self-hosted engine
Learning curve for configuration
BDurable Functions / State Machine Code
Full developer control and testability
Native cloud stateful execution
No visual process editor for non-devs
Changes require code deployment
CCRM-Driven Workflow (MS Dynamics)
Already in use via BLISS, familiar
Officers already trained on it
Tightly coupled to CRM platform
Limited cross-registry orchestration
8
Lifecycle states
SLA
Per state clock
Auto
Escalation
UNDER EVALUATION →Option A or B depends on team capability and process stability
SXM CONTEXT
Currently applications stall with no automatic escalation. Officers manage deadlines manually. There is no appeal state machine — citizens who appeal have no formal tracking. The 8-state model formalises what already happens informally, adding SLA accountability.
SLA CLOCK = ACCOUNTABILITY
The 75% alert means officers are warned before a breach, not after. Automatic escalation at 100% removes the need for manual supervisor monitoring. In comparable programmes this reduced SLA breaches by 60% within the first quarter of operation.
OPTION C RISK
CRM-driven workflow (Dynamics) locks the lifecycle inside the CRM — registry adapters, notifications, and payments cannot trigger state transitions directly. This creates a bottleneck where every event must route through the CRM, adding latency and a single point of failure.
PROCESS STABILITY FIRST
Choose Option A (BPMN) if service process definitions will change frequently — no-code updates are essential. Choose Option B (code) if processes are stable and the team is confident in the state machine — it gives finer control and easier testing.
PROPOSED 8-STATE LIFECYCLE — OPTION A/B (ILLUSTRATIVE)
PROPOSED STAGE 7 / 10
Decision 06 — Citizen Happiness IndexQ6 of 6

Measure What Citizens Actually Experience.

USER STORY"As a government director, I want a daily score showing whether citizens are satisfied — based on real behaviour and outcomes, not just an annual survey."
OPTIONS EVALUATED
AMulti-Signal CXI (4 sources combined)
Behavioural + NPS + SLA + AI signals
Daily refresh, live dashboard per role
Requires APM SDK in citizen SPA
Signal weighting needs calibration
BTransactional NPS Only
Simple to implement and understand
Low technical dependency
Depends on citizen response rate
No behavioural signal captured
CAnnual Citizen Survey (traditional)
Established, benchmarkable methodology
No technical dependency at all
Annual — problems found far too late
No real-time operational signal
4
Signal types
Daily
Score refresh
3
Audience levels
PROPOSED →Multi-signal CXI via Power BI Embedded + App Insights — weights to agree with DGTP
SXM CONTEXT
Sint Maarten has no current CX measurement for digital government services. The annual survey last ran in 2021. Without a live score, the Steering Committee cannot demonstrate programme value, identify failing services, or justify continued investment.
PROGRAMME JUSTIFICATION
A daily CXI score is the single most powerful tool for securing continued programme funding. When the score improves from 60 to 78, that number tells the story — no narrative required. Target: CXI ≥ 80 within 12 months of full deployment.
OPTION C IS TOO LATE
An annual survey discovers problems 6–18 months after they occur — too late to act on within the programme. If a service drops in quality in Month 3, Option C finds out in Month 14 at the next survey cycle. Option A detects the same drop within 24 hours.
SIGNAL WEIGHT GUIDANCE
Suggested starting weights: NPS 35% (citizen voice), Behavioural APM 25% (actual usage), SLA compliance 25% (operational delivery), Proactive AI acceptance 15% (anticipation quality). Review and recalibrate after 90 days of baseline data.
PROPOSED CXI ARCHITECTURE — OPTION A (ILLUSTRATIVE)
PROPOSED STAGE 8 / 10
SummaryAll six decisions compared

Decision Matrix — Six Questions at a Glance

All options remain open. Updated to reflect QConnect for Q2, pull-based notifications in Q3, and revised implementation sequencing.

DECISION OPTION A OPTION B OPTION C KEY CRITERIA COMPLEXITY OPS BURDEN PROPOSED
01 Data Bus ★ Azure APIM + SB WSO2 APIM X-Road Cloud strategy · Ops capacity · No RabbitMQ/Kafka — Azure SB only Medium Low Azure APIM + Service Bus
02 Appointments ★ QConnect (Qmatic) MS Bookings Custom Booking API Qmatic already in place · SMS two-way · QR kiosk · Case linkage via correlationId Low Low QConnect Bridge
03 Notifications ★ Azure ACS Twilio / SendGrid Gov Notify Module Push (Email · SMS · FCM) + Pull (In-App SSE /live) · Audit trail · EN + PAP Low Low Azure ACS + SSE Pull
04 Traceability ★ Event Sourcing + WORM RDBMS Audit Table DLT / Blockchain Legal immutability · correlationId end-to-end · 7-yr retention High Low WORM + App Insights
05 Lifecycle ★ BPMN Engine Durable Functions CRM Workflow 8-state lifecycle · SLA clocks · Auto-escalation · Team BPMN skill Medium Medium BPMN / Durable Fn
06 Happiness ★ Multi-signal CXI NPS Only Annual Survey NPS 35% · APM 25% · SLA 25% · AI 15% · Target ≥ 80 Medium Low Power BI + App Insights
REVISED SEQUENCING — WHAT TO DECIDE FIRST
1ST Q1 Data Bus — APIM + Service Bus. Everything else routes through it
2ND Q5 Lifecycle — BPMN defines the 8 states that Q4 events track
3RD Q3 + Q4 — Push + Pull notifications and WORM audit share the event bus
4TH Q2 QConnect — Qmatic bridge, can run in parallel after Q1
5TH Q6 CXI — needs live signals from Q3, Q4, Q5 first
DECISION INTERDEPENDENCIES
Q1 → Q2 (QConnect via APIM) · Q3 (notification routing) · Q4 (event bus) · Q5 (orchestration)
Q5 → Q4 (state transitions emit WORM events) · Q6 (SLA compliance signal)
Q3 → Push (ACS) + Pull (SSE /live) · Q6 (NPS via SMS) · Q2 (QConnect reminders)
Q4 → Q6 (behavioural APM signal) · Q5 (WORM event per state transition)
Q2 QConnect → Q3 (booking reminders via ACS) · Q5 (appointment advances case state)
Q6 requires Q1 + Q3 (push+pull) + Q4 + Q5 all live to produce a meaningful CXI score
ACTIONS NEEDED FROM DGTP TODAY
□ Confirm cloud strategy — Azure-native confirmed; no RabbitMQ/Kafka
□ Confirm Qmatic API access for QConnect bridge (Q2) integration
□ Sign off pull-based SSE /live endpoint for citizen portal (Q3)
□ Legal sign-off on 7-year WORM retention for Q4 audit events
□ Confirm BPMN vs Durable Functions skill set for Q5 team
□ Agree CXI signal weights and ≥ 80 target before baseline starts (Q6)

Appendix · 17 of 17 Key Terminology & Abbreviations

Abbreviations & Key Terms

Click any category to expand. All acronyms and terms used throughout this deck.

01 Programme & Architecture DGTP · OGF · GovMap · EA · Q1–Q6 · CXI · NPS · SLA · BPMN · DTO ▾
DGTPDigital Government Transformation Project — the overarching programme for Sint Maarten's government digitalisation
OGFOneGov Framework — Orange BD's whole-of-government digital platform powering citizen and officer portals
GovMapThe DGTP architecture decision brief — this document. Maps six architectural decisions to outcomes.
EAEnterprise Architecture — the practice of aligning technology decisions with business strategy and governance
Q1–Q6The six architecture decisions: Data Bus · Appointments · Notifications · Traceability · Lifecycle · Happiness
CXICitizen Experience Index — composite daily score: NPS 35% + APM 25% + SLA 25% + AI Acceptance 15%
NPSNet Promoter Score — citizen satisfaction survey sent 2 hours post-service completion via SMS
SLAService Level Agreement — time-bound commitment for processing a government service application
BPMNBusiness Process Model and Notation — standard used to model the 8-state application lifecycle (Q5)
DTOData Transfer Object — canonical data model normalising registry responses across all 6 protocols
02 Azure & Integration Stack APIM · Service Bus · ACS · AKS · WAF · WORM · DLQ · PIM · SSE · IaC ▾
APIMAzure API Management — Q1 gateway. JWT/PKCE validation, rate limiting, SOAP→REST transform, mock policies
Service BusAzure Service Bus Premium — Q1 event backbone. Ordered delivery, retry, DLQ, circuit breaker, ETag invalidation. No Kafka/RabbitMQ.
ACSAzure Communication Services — Q3 notification engine. Email (@gov.sx), SMS (two-way), FCM Push, In-App SSE
AKSAzure Kubernetes Service — container orchestration platform hosting all DGTP microservices
WAFWeb Application Firewall — OWASP ruleset at Azure Front Door. Blocks injection, XSS, OWASP Top 10
WORMWrite-Once Read-Many — Q4 immutable audit storage. 7-year legal retention. No deletion by anyone, ever.
DLQDead-Letter Queue — Service Bus queue for messages that fail after maximum retry attempts
PIMPrivileged Identity Management — Entra ID JIT access for officers. No standing admin privileges.
SSEServer-Sent Events — pull-based notification channel. Citizen portal subscribes to /live endpoint for real-time updates
IaCInfrastructure as Code — all Azure environments in Bicep + Terraform, deployed via GitHub Actions CI/CD
03 Registries, Protocols & Patterns CASBES · COCI · BLISS · SIAH · StUF-BG · NSN · CRIB · ABAC · correlationId · QConnect ▾
CASBESCivil Affairs System — Sint Maarten Civil Registry. StUF-BG SOAP protocol. NSN is the primary citizen key.
COCIChamber of Commerce — Business Registry. Oracle OData REST. CRIB number is the primary business identifier.
BLISSBusiness Licence Information System — Dynamics 365 OData v4. Statuses: ACTIVE · SUSPENDED · EXPIRED · PENDING
SIAHTax Administration — SFTP daily batch file processed via Azure Logic Apps. CRIB cross-reference active.
StUF-BGStandard Exchange Format Municipalities — Dutch SOAP standard used by Civil Registry. Transformed to REST at APIM.
NSNNational Service Number — primary citizen identifier (P1). Carried in JWT and all correlationId chains.
CRIBBusiness identifier (B8) used across Business Registry and Tax Administration cross-references
ABACAttribute-Based Access Control — enforced at BFF. NSN in JWT must match the application being accessed.
correlationIdUnique UUID assigned at submission. Links every event, registry call, notification, and audit record across one citizen journey.
QConnectQ2 bridge to Qmatic. Adds SMS two-way rescheduling, QR kiosk check-in, D-1 reminders. Qmatic itself is not replaced.
GOVMAP · DGTP SINT MAARTEN
